Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 65/5/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 29 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:100

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper contains 29 questions divided into four sections A, B, C and D. Section A comprises of 4 questions of one mark each, Section B comprises of 8 questions of two marks each, Section C comprises of 11 questions of four marks each and Section D comprises of 6 questions of six marks each.
  3. All questions in Section A are to be answered in one word, one sentence or as per the exact requirement of the question.
  4. There is no overall choice. However, internal choice has been provided in 1 question of Section A, 3 questions of Section B, 3 questions of Section C and 3 questions of Section D. You have to attempt only one of the alternatives in all such questions.
  5. Use of calculators is not permitted. You may ask for logarithmic tables, if required.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 4 carry 1 mark each.

1.

Find the angle between the line {tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+3 \hat{k})+\lambda(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} and the plane {tex} \vec{r} \cdot(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k})=3{/tex}.

OR

Find the co-ordinates of the point, where the line {tex}\frac{x+2}{1}=\frac{y-5}{3}=\frac{z+1}{5}{/tex} cuts the yz-plane.
2.

If y = 5e7x + 6e-7x, show that {tex}\frac{{d}^{2} {y}}{{dx}^{2}}{/tex} = 49y
3.

If A is a square matrix of order 2 and {tex}|A|{/tex} = 4, then find the value of {tex}|2.AA'|{/tex}, where A' is the transpose of matrix A.
4.

Find the order of the differential equation of the family of circles of radius 3 units.

SECTION B

Question numbers 5 to 12 carry 2 marks each.

5.

Find the value of (x - y) from the matrix equation {tex}2\left[\begin{array}{cc} x & 5 \\ 7 & y-3 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & -4 \\ 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 7 & 6 \\ 15 & 14 \end{array}\right]{/tex}.
6.

Solve the following differential equation.
{tex} \left( y + 3 x ^ { 2 } \right) \frac { d x } { d y } = x{/tex}
7.

Find: {tex}\int{/tex} ex(cos x - sin x)cosec2 xdx 
8.

Using vectors, prove that the points (2, -1, 3), (3, -5, 1) and (-1, 11, 9) are collinear.

OR

For any two vectors {tex}\vec{a} {/tex} and {tex}\vec{b}{/tex}, prove that {tex}(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})^{2}=\vec{{a}^{2}} \vec{{b}^{2}}-(\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b})^{2}{/tex}.
9.

Find: {tex}\int \frac{x-1}{(x-2)(x-3)} d x {/tex}

OR

Integrate: {tex}\frac{e^x}{\sqrt{5-4 e^x-e^{2 x}}}{/tex} with respect to x.
10.

If P(A) = 0.6, P(B) = 0.5 and P(B|A) = 0.4, find P(A {tex}\cup{/tex} B) and P(A|B).
11.

If an operation * on the set of integers Z is defined by a * b = 2a2 + b, then find

  1. whether it is a binary or not, and
  2. if a binary, then is it commutative or not.
12.

Four cards are drawn one by one with replacement from a well-shuffled deck of playing cards. Find the probability that at least three cards are of diamonds.

OR

The probability of two students A and B coming to school on time are {tex}\frac {2} {7}{/tex} and {tex}\frac {4} {7}{/tex}, respectively. Assuming that the events A coming on time and B coming on time are independent, find the probability of only one of them coming to school on time.

SECTION C

Question numbers 13 to 23 carry 4 marks each.

13.

If xpyq = (x + y)p+q, prove that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{y}{x}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}=0{/tex}.
14.

Find: {tex}\int(\sin x \cdot \sin 2 x \cdot \sin 3 x) d x{/tex}
15.

Differentiate {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{3 \mathrm{x}-\mathrm{x}^3}{1-3 \mathrm{x}^2},|\mathrm{x}|<\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} w.r.t. {tex}\tan ^{-1} \frac{x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}}{/tex}.

OR

If {tex}\sqrt{1-x^2}+\sqrt{1-y^2}{/tex} = a (x - y), |x| < 1, |y| < 1, show that {tex}\frac{d y}{d x}=\sqrt{\frac{1-y^2}{1-x^2}}{/tex}.
16.

Find the particular solution of the differential equation: (1 + e2x) dy + (1 + y2) ex dx = 0, given that y(0) = 1.

OR

Find the particular solution of the differential equation: {tex}x \frac{d y}{d x} \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)+x-y \sin \left(\frac{y}{x}\right)=0{/tex}, given that {tex}y(1)=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}.
17.

Prove that the relation R in the set A = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7} given by R = {(a, b) : |a - b| is even} is an equivalence relation.

OR

Show that the function f in A = R -{tex}\left\{ \frac { 2 } { 3 } \right\}{/tex} defined as f (x) ={tex}\frac { 4 x + 3 } { 6 x - 4 }{/tex} is one-one and onto. Hence, find f-1.
18.

Find whether the function {tex}f(x)=\cos \left(2 x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right){/tex}; is increasing or decreasing in the interval {tex}\frac{3 \pi}{8}<x<\frac{5 \pi}{8}{/tex}.
19.

Find the equation of the plane passing through the point (-1, 3, 2) and perpendicular to the planes x + 2y + 3z = 5 and 3x + 3y + z = 0.
20.

Prove that: {tex}\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{5}{12}+\cos ^{-1} \frac{63}{65}=\frac{\pi}{2}{/tex}
21.

Evaluate: {tex}\int\limits_1^5(|x-1|+|x-2|+|x-4|) d x{/tex}
22.

Using vectors, find the value of x such that the four points A(x, 5, -1), B(3, 2, 1), C(4, 5, 5) and D(4, 2, -2) are coplanar.
23.

If x, y, z are different and {tex}\Delta=\left|\begin{array}{lll} x & x^{2} & x^{3}-1 \\ y & y^{2} & y^{3}-1 \\ z & z^{2} & z^{3}-1 \end{array}\right|=0{/tex}, then using properties of determinants, show that xyz = 1

SECTION D

Question numbers 24 to 29 carry 6 marks each.

24.

Using integration, find the area of the following region: {(x, y) : x2 + y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 16a2 and y2 {tex}\leq{/tex} 6ax}

OR

Using integration, find the area of the region bounded by the triangle ABC when its sides are given by the lines 4x - y + 5 = 0, x + y - 5 = 0 and x - 4y + 5 = 0.
25.

Find the vector equation of the line passing through (2, 1, -1) and parallel to the line {tex}\vec{r}=(\hat{i}+\hat{j})+\lambda(2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}){/tex}. Also, find the distance between these two lines.

OR

Find the coordinates of the foot Q of the perpendicular drawn from the point P(1, 3, 4) to the plane 2x - y + z + 3 = 0. Find the distance PQ and the image of P treating the plane as a mirror.
26.

A company manufactures two types of novelty souvenirs made of plywood. Souvenirs of type A requires 5 minutes each for cutting and 10 minutes each for assembling. Souvenirs of type B require 8 minutes each for cutting and 8 minutes each for assembling. Given that total time for cutting is 3 hours 20 minutes and for assembling 4 hours. The profit for type A souvenir ₹ 100 each and for type B souvenir, profit is ₹120 each. How many souvenirs of each type should the company manufacture in order to maximize the profit? Formulate the problem as an LPP and solve it graphically.
27.

In answering a question on a multiple choice questions test with four choices in each question, out of which only one is correct, a student either guesses or copies or knows the answer. The probability that he makes a guess is {tex}\frac {1} {4}{/tex} and the probability the he copies is also {tex}\frac {1} {4}{/tex}. The probability that the answer is correct, given that he copied it is {tex}\frac {3} {4}{/tex}. Find the probability that he knows the answer to the question, given that he correctly answered it.
28.

An isosceles triangle of vertical angle 2{tex}\theta{/tex} is inscribed in a circle of radius a. Show that the area of the triangle is maximum when {tex}\theta=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}.
29.

Using elementary row transformations, find the inverse of the matrix {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 0 & -1 \\ 2 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}.

OR

Using matrices, solve the following system of linear equations:
2x + 3y + 10z = 4
4x - 6y + 5z = 1
6x + 9y - 20z = 2

Annual Exam - 2019

Code: 65/5/1 (set-1)

  • Please check that this question paper contains 11 printed pages.
  • Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  • Please check that this question paper contains 29 questions.
  • Please write down the Serial Number of the question before attempting it.
  • 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

MATHEMATICS

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:100

General Instructions :

  1. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper contains 29 questions divided into four sections A, B, C and D. Section A comprises of 4 questions of one mark each, Section B comprises of 8 questions of two marks each, Section C comprises of 11 questions of four marks each and Section D comprises of 6 questions of six marks each.
  3. All questions in Section A are to be answered in one word, one sentence or as per the exact requirement of the question.
  4. There is no overall choice. However, internal choice has been provided in 1 question of Section A, 3 questions of Section B, 3 questions of Section C and 3 questions of Section D. You have to attempt only one of the alternatives in all such questions.
  5. Use of calculators is not permitted. You may ask for logarithmic tables, if required.

SECTION A

Question numbers 1 to 4 carry 1 mark each.

1.

Find the angle between the line {tex}\vec{r}=(2 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+3 \hat{k})+\lambda(3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}){/tex} and the plane {tex} \vec{r} \cdot(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k})=3{/tex}.

Solution:

We know that the angle {tex}\theta{/tex} between the line {tex}\vec{r}=\vec{r}_{1}+\lambda \vec{m}{/tex} and the plane {tex}\vec{r} \vec{n}=q{/tex} is given by  {tex}Sin\theta=\left|\frac{\vec{m} \vec{n}}{|\vec{m}||\vec n|}\right|{/tex} Here, {tex}\vec{m}=3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k} {/tex} and {tex}\vec{n}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \sin \theta{/tex} = {tex}\left|\frac{(3 i-j+2 \vec{k})(i+\hat{j}+\hat{k})}{\left.|3 \hat{i}-\hat{j}+2 \hat{k}|\right|^{2}+\hat{j}+\hat{k} |}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\left|\frac{3 \times 1+(-1) \times 1+2 \times 1}{| \sqrt{3^{2}+(-1)^{2}+2^{2}} \sqrt{1^{2}+1^{2}+1^{2}}}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\left|\frac{3-1+2}{\sqrt{14} \sqrt{3}}\right|=\frac{4}{\sqrt{42}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \theta=\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{\sqrt{42}}{/tex} there, the angle between the line and the plane is {tex}\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{\sqrt{42}}{/tex}.

 1

OR

Find the co-ordinates of the point, where the line {tex}\frac{x+2}{1}=\frac{y-5}{3}=\frac{z+1}{5}{/tex} cuts the yz-plane.

Solution:

Any point on {tex}\frac{x+2}{1}=\frac{y-5}{3}=\frac{z+1}{5}{/tex}= {tex}\lambda{/tex} ... (i)
is {tex}(\lambda-2,3 \lambda+5,5 \lambda-1){/tex}
Line (i) cuts yz plane at {tex}\lambda-2{/tex} = 0 i.e, {tex}\lambda = 2{/tex}
Therefore, the required points is(0, 11, 9)

 1
2.

If y = 5e7x + 6e-7x, show that {tex}\frac{{d}^{2} {y}}{{dx}^{2}}{/tex} = 49y

Solution:

Given : y = 5e7x + 6e-7x 
Then, we have
{tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = {tex}5 \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{7 x}\right){/tex} + {tex}6 \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{-7 x}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}5 \cdot e^{7 x} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(7 x){/tex} + {tex}6 \cdot e^{-7 x} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(-7 x){/tex} 
= 35e7x - 42e-7x 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{d^{2} y}{d x^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}35 \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{7 x}\right){/tex} - {tex}42 \cdot \frac{d}{d x}\left(e^{-7 x}\right){/tex} 
= {tex}35 \cdot e^{7} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(7 x){/tex} - {tex}42 \cdot e^{-7 x} \cdot \frac{d}{d x}(-7 x){/tex} 
= 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 35 e7x + 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 42 e-7x 
= 49 {tex}\times{/tex} 5e7x + 49 {tex}\times{/tex} 6e-7x 
= 49(5 e7x + 6 e-7x)
= 49y
Henced proved

 1
3.

If A is a square matrix of order 2 and {tex}|A|{/tex} = 4, then find the value of {tex}|2.AA'|{/tex}, where A' is the transpose of matrix A.

Solution:

We can write {tex}|2.AA'|{/tex} = 4{tex}|A|{/tex}{tex}|A'|{/tex}
= 4{tex}\times{/tex}4{tex}\times{/tex}4 = 64

 1
4.

Find the order of the differential equation of the family of circles of radius 3 units.

Solution:

The equation of the family of circles having centre (0, b) on y -axis and radius 3 units is
x2 + (y - b)2 = 9 .....(i)
Differentiating both sides w.r.t. x, we get
2x + 2(y - b){tex}\frac{d y}{d x}{/tex} = 0 or y - b = {tex}-\frac{x}{\frac{d y}{d x}}{/tex} 
Putting this value of y - b in (i), we get
{tex}x^{2}+\frac{x^{2}}{\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}}{/tex} = 9
or {tex}\left(x^{2}-9\right)+\frac{x^{2}}{\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}}=0{/tex} 
or {tex}\left(x^{2}-9\right)\left(\frac{d y}{d x}\right)^{2}+x^{2}{/tex} = 0
The order of the differential equation is 2.

 1

SECTION B

Question numbers 5 to 12 carry 2 marks each.

5.

Find the value of (x - y) from the matrix equation {tex}2\left[\begin{array}{cc} x & 5 \\ 7 & y-3 \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{cc} -3 & -4 \\ 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 7 & 6 \\ 15 & 14 \end{array}\right]{/tex}.

Solution:

{tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}2 x-3 & 6 \\ 15 & 2 y-4\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc}7 & 6 \\ 15 & 14\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x - 3 = 7 and 2y - 4 = 14 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 5, y = 9 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x - y = -4

 2
6.

Solve the following differential equation.
{tex} \left( y + 3 x ^ { 2 } \right) \frac { d x } { d y } = x{/tex}

Solution:

According to the question,we have to solve the differential equation ,
{tex} \left( y + 3 x ^ { 2 } \right) \frac { d x } { d y } = x \Rightarrow \frac { d y } { d x } = \frac { y } { x } + 3 x{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \quad \frac { d y } { d x } - \frac { y } { x } = 3 x{/tex}
which is a linear differential equation of the form
{tex} \frac { d y } { d x } + P y = Q{/tex}.
Here, {tex} P = \frac { - 1 } { x }{/tex}and Q = 3x

In answering a question on a multiple choice questions test with four choices in each question, out of which only one is correct, a student either guesses or copies or knows the answer. The probability that he makes a guess is {tex}\frac {1} {4}{/tex} and the probability the he copies is also {tex}\frac {1} {4}{/tex}. The probability that the answer is correct, given that he copied it is {tex}\frac {3} {4}{/tex}. Find the probability that he knows the answer to the question, given that he correctly answered it.

Solution:

Let E1 = Student guesses the answer
E2 = Student copies the answer
E3 = Student knows the answer
A = Student answers the question correctly.
{tex}\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_1\right)=\frac{1}{4}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_2\right)=\frac{1}{4}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_3\right)=1-\left(\frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{4}\right)=\frac{1}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A} \mid \mathrm{E}_1\right)=\frac{1}{4}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A} \mid \mathrm{E}_2\right)=\frac{3}{4}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A} \mid \mathrm{E}_3\right)=1{/tex}
The required probability
{tex}=P\left(E_3 \mid A\right)=\frac{P\left(E_3\right) \times P\left(A \mid E_3\right)}{\sum\limits_{i=1}^3 P\left(E_i\right) \times P\left(A \mid E_i\right)}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\frac{1}{2} \times 1}{\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{3}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \times 1}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\frac{1}{8}+\frac{3}{8}+1}=\frac{8}{12}=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

An isosceles triangle of vertical angle 2{tex}\theta{/tex} is inscribed in a circle of radius a. Show that the area of the triangle is maximum when {tex}\theta=\frac{\pi}{6}{/tex}.

Solution:

Let ABC be an isosceles triangle inscribed in the circle with radius a such that AB = AC.
{tex}AD = AO + OD = a + a\cos 2\theta{/tex}and {tex}BC = 2BD = 2a\sin 2\theta{/tex} (see fig.)

Therefore, area of the triangle ABC i.e. {tex}\triangle = \frac{1}{2}BC.AD{/tex} 
{tex}= \frac{1}{2}2a\sin 2\theta .\left( {a + a\cos 2\theta } \right){/tex} 
{tex}= {a^2}\sin 2\theta \left( {1 + \cos 2\theta } \right){/tex} 
{tex} \Rightarrow \triangle = {a^2}\sin 2\theta + \frac{1}{2}{a^2}\sin 4\theta {/tex}
Therefore, {tex}\frac{{d\Delta }}{{d\theta }} = 2{a^2}\cos 2\theta + 2{a^2}\cos 4\theta {/tex} 
{tex} = 2{a^2}\left( {\cos 2\theta + \cos 4\theta } \right){/tex} 
{tex}\frac{{d\Delta }}{{d\theta }} = 0 \Rightarrow \cos 2\theta = - \cos 4\theta = \cos \left( {\pi - 4\theta } \right){/tex} 
Therefore, {tex}2\theta = \pi - 4\theta \Rightarrow \theta = \frac{\pi }{6}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{{{d^2}\Delta }}{{d{\theta ^2}}} = 2{a^2}\left( { - 2\sin 2\theta - 4\sin 4\theta } \right) < 0\left( {at\ \theta = \frac{\pi }{6}} \right){/tex}
Therefore, Area of triangle is maximum when {tex}\theta = \frac{\pi }{6}.{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right] A{/tex}
 Applying  R1 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R1 -R2 
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -3 & -1 \\ 0 & 9 & 2 \\ 0 & 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right] \cdot A{/tex}
 Applying  R2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R2 - 2R1 
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -3 & -1 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 4 & 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ -2 & 3 & -2 \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right] \cdot A{/tex}
Applying  R1 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R1 + 3R2, R3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R3 - 4R2,
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -5 & 8 & -6 \\ -2 & 3 & -2 \\ 8 & -12 & 9 \end{array}\right] \cdot A{/tex}
Applying  R1 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R1 + R3, 
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]={/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 3 \\ -2 & 3 & -2 \\ 8 & -12 & 9 \end{array}\right] \cdot A{/tex}
Hence, {tex}A^{-1}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -4 & 3 \\ -2 & 3 & -2 \\ 8 & -12 & 9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} is the inverse matrix of matrix A.

In answering a question on a multiple choice questions test with four choices in each question, out of which only one is correct, a student either guesses or copies or knows the answer. The probability that he makes a guess is {tex}\frac {1} {4}{/tex} and the probability the he copies is also {tex}\frac {1} {4}{/tex} . The probability that the answer is correct, given that he copied it is {tex}\frac {3} {4}{/tex} . Find the probability that he knows the answer to the question, given that he correctly answered it. Solution: Let E 1 = Student guesses the answer E 2 = Student copies the answer E 3 = Student knows the answer A = Student answers the question correctly. {tex}\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_1\right)=\frac{1}{4}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_2\right)=\frac{1}{4}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{E}_3\right)=1-\left(\frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{4}\right)=\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A} \mid \mathrm{E}_1\right)=\frac{1}{4}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A} \mid \mathrm{E}_2\right)=\frac{3}{4}, \mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{A} \mid \mathrm{E}_3\right)=1{/tex} The required probability {tex}=P\left(E_3 \mid A\right)=\frac{P\left(E_3\right) \times P\left(A \mid E_3\right)}{\sum\limits_{i=1}^3 P\left(E_i\right) \times P\left(A \mid E_i\right)}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{\frac{1}{2} \times 1}{\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{3}{4}+\frac{1}{2} \times 1}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{\frac{1}{8}+\frac{3}{8}+1}=\frac{8}{12}=\frac{2}{3}{/tex} 6 28.
